display.title: Australian women and the Great War : aspects of ideological change with particular emphasis on Queensland
display.abstract: THE WOMANI am the Woman. | Slave of a slave I stand | In every land. A 'wage slave is the Man - | Sustenance slave am I. | Such is the tie.We .are enthralled | By the very deeds we do. | Both me and you.You fight for freedom: | That all men may be | From Bondage free:Free to enjoy, Free |To possess that which they make | Their wealth to take!While in all lands | 'Gainst slavery you rave you love your slave! | Can you gain freedom While I am not free?It cannot be. | From womb of slave No freeman can be bred, | But slave instead.If you want free men, | Men who are not afraid | Of laws man-made: Not afraid of God, | Nor heaven nor hell; | Who will not sell For gold the birthright | To the wealth they make - | Then you must takeYour bondage from us; | Let your -woman knoe (sic) | Their chains must goI AM THE WOMAN; | SLAVE OF A SLAVE I STAND | IN EVERY LAND.Jennie Scott Griffiths - Feminist, Pacifist and Socialist during the Great War in Australia.

marc.label: Title
marc.value: Australian women and the Great War : aspects of ideological change with particular emphasis on Queensland
